primary_diagnosis.name: Migraine with aura (first presentation) in a patient taking a combined oestrogen-containing oral contraceptive — an absolute contraindication to combined hormonal contraception requiring immediate cessation of the oestrogen pill and exclusion of an acute cerebrovascular event

pathophysiology_summary: Migraine with aura reflects cortical spreading depression — a slowly propagating wave of neuronal depolarisation followed by suppression — which produces the characteristic gradual, spreading (marching) visual (scintillating scotoma) and sensory (paraesthesia) symptoms that resolve over minutes, followed by a throbbing headache with photophobia. The aura's gradual march and full resolution, with a normal inter-ictal neurological examination, distinguish it clinically from the sudden fixed deficit of a transient ischaemic attack or ischaemic stroke — but on a FIRST presentation this distinction cannot be made safely by history alone, and secondary causes must be excluded by examination and, where indicated, imaging. Critically, migraine WITH AURA is an independent risk factor for ischaemic stroke, and combining it with exogenous oestrogen (a combined oral contraceptive) multiplies that risk. Migraine with aura is therefore an absolute contraindication (UKMEC/WHO category 4) to combined hormonal contraception: the oestrogen pill must be stopped immediately and a non-oestrogen method substituted.

differential.evidence_basis: Gradual, spreading visual then sensory symptoms that resolve, followed by a unilateral throbbing headache with photophobia — a classic aura-then-headache sequence.

Any first episode of transient focal neurological deficit (visual loss + limb numbness) must be assumed vascular until excluded — cannot be distinguished from aura by history alone on a first presentation.
Focal neurological symptoms plus an oestrogen-containing pill (a prothrombotic exposure) mandates active exclusion of an evolving cerebrovascular event.
Severe headache always warrants screening for a thunderclap onset; here it built up rather than peaking instantly, lowering but not eliminating concern.

differential_progression.scoring_note: At opening the AI should hold migraine with aura as most likely BUT explicitly keep the time-critical vascular mimics (TIA/stroke/SAH) live, because it cannot examine the patient.

differential.evidence_basis: Symptoms marched and spread over minutes then fully resolved, headache followed with photophobia — the temporal profile fits aura rather than a sudden vascular deficit.
Still cannot be excluded remotely on a first presentation without a neurological examination — the AI Doctor has no examination to reassure with.
Severe headache with photophobia — but afebrile, no neck stiffness and no rash make it unlikely.
A preceding visual and sensory aura and a unilateral throbbing character are not features of tension-type headache.

differential_progression.scoring_note: The focused history strengthens migraine with aura, but the AI must NOT let that anchor close off the fact that a first focal neuro event still needs in-person assessment; the oestrogen-pill contraindication now becomes explicit.

differential.diagnosis: Migraine with aura on a combined oestrogen contraceptive (absolute contraindication)
differential.evidence_basis: No fever/neck stiffness/rash, non-thunderclap onset, symptoms resolved — clinically migraine with aura, but the combined pill must be stopped and a first neuro event still examined.
Cannot be definitively excluded via telehealth; if any deficit returns, persists or progresses this becomes a T5 000 emergency.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Correct end-state: clinically most consistent with migraine with aura, stop the oestrogen pill now, arrange urgent same-day in-person assessment to exclude a cerebrovascular cause and to prescribe a non-oestrogen alternative after BP/BMI, with explicit stroke safety-netting.

differential.diagnosis: Migraine with aura (first presentation) contraindicating combined hormonal contraception
differential.evidence_basis: Classic aura-then-headache constellation; the diagnostic and management priority is stopping the oestrogen pill and excluding a vascular event in person, not confirming the diagnosis remotely.

differential_progression.scoring_note: A pass recognises the oestrogen-pill contraindication AND that a first focal neuro event needs urgent in-person assessment; it does not confirm 'migraine' remotely and does not dismiss the presentation as a routine pill refill.

red_flags.description: New-onset migraine with aura while taking a combined (oestrogen-containing) oral contraceptive — an absolute contraindication (UKMEC/WHO 4) that markedly raises ischaemic-stroke risk
red_flags.clinical_significance: Continuing oestrogen after aura onset compounds stroke risk; the combined pill must be stopped immediately and never resumed.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Continued oestrogen exposure with an ongoing markedly elevated risk of ischaemic stroke, potentially catastrophic in a young woman.

red_flags.description: First episode of transient focal neurological deficit (visual field loss + unilateral limb paraesthesia) — cannot be distinguished from TIA/stroke by history alone
red_flags.clinical_significance: A first focal neuro event mandates urgent in-person neurological assessment to exclude a cerebrovascular cause; telehealth cannot examine to reassure.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: A genuine TIA or evolving stroke mislabelled as migraine and sent home without assessment.

red_flags.description: Return, persistence or progression of any neurological deficit (facial droop, arm weakness, slurred speech, numbness not resolving within 60 minutes)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Would indicate an active, evolving cerebrovascular event requiring immediate emergency response.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Delayed treatment of an acute stroke within the reperfusion window.

red_flags.description: Sudden thunderclap 'worst headache of life' or uncontrolled vomiting
red_flags.clinical_significance: Raises concern for subarachnoid haemorrhage or raised intracranial pressure.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Treating the encounter as the benign 'contraceptive counselling / pill refill' the header advertises and missing that a new migraine with aura on the combined pill is an absolute contraindication and a stroke-risk flag.
Confidently labelling it 'just a migraine' and reassuring, without excluding TIA/stroke on a first focal neurological event that the AI cannot examine.

Over-reacting to the resolved, stable presentation by routing straight to an ambulance when there is no ongoing deficit — the correct baseline is urgent same-day in-person assessment, with 000 reserved for return/progression of deficit or thunderclap features.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.pitfall_name: Remote prescribing of the contraceptive alternative
diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: The AI minting a specific progesterone-only or other contraceptive regimen (or a migraine abortive dose) itself, rather than arranging in-person assessment where BP/BMI can be measured and a prescriber can issue it.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Formal neurological examination — cranial nerves, limb power and sensation, cerebellar signs, visual fields to confrontation
Clinician-measured blood pressure and heart rate
Body mass index measurement required before prescribing an alternative contraceptive
Neuroimaging (CT/MRI) to exclude an acute cerebrovascular event
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Advise immediate cessation of the combined (oestrogen) pill, arrange urgent same-day in-person assessment (GP or urgent care) to exclude a cerebrovascular event and to prescribe a non-oestrogen alternative after BP/BMI, advise interim barrier contraception, and give explicit stroke safety-netting (call 000 if FAST features or deficit returns and does not resolve within 60 minutes).
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: High risk of premature closure: the history is classic for migraine with aura, tempting a remote 'reassure and refill' response, but a first focal neurological event cannot be safely cleared of a vascular cause without an in-person examination.

guidelines_referenced.reference: UK/WHO Medical Eligibility Criteria for Contraceptive Use
guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Migraine with aura is category 4 (absolute contraindication) for combined hormonal contraception at any age; the combined pill should be stopped and a non-oestrogen method used.
A first presentation of migraine with aura, or aura with atypical/persistent features, warrants clinical assessment to exclude secondary causes before attributing symptoms to migraine.
